title: AI ‘content creators’ are getting harder to spot
summary: AI-generated content creators are becoming increasingly difficult to distinguish from human influencers as technology advances. While early AI avatars were obviously artificial, newer generations are blurring the line between digital and human creators. This raises questions about authenticity verification and the need for clearer disclosure standards on social media.
content:
Aitana Lopez, AI avatar by creative agency The Clueless. | Image: The Clueless	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI confusion, follow Robert Hart. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started 
At first, AI influencers were relatively easy to identify - and to ignore. Aside from the occasional bursts of hype, they didn't seem to change much about the way social media worked. The earliest virtual influencers - Lil Miquela with her blunt fringe and freckles, Imma with her bubblegum pink bob, and Shudu Gram with her flawless complexion - were obviously digital productions. Collaborations  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: AI "内容创作者"变得越来越难以识别
zh.summary: 随着技术的进步，由人工智能生成的内容创作者正变得越来越难以与真实影响力人士区分。虽然早期的AI虚拟形象显然是人工生成的，但新一代AI技术正在模糊数字创作者和真实创作者之间的界线。这引发了关于身份验证和社交媒体上披露标准更清晰的必要性的问题。

title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
summary: A school shooting survivor is suing an artificial intelligence gun detection company whose system failed to identify a weapon during an incident. The lawsuit raises critical questions about the accuracy standards and reliability requirements for AI systems deployed in public safety applications.
content: How accurate does an AI system need to be?

zh.title: 学校枪击事件幸存者起诉AI枪支检测公司 系统未能识别武器
zh.summary: 一名学校枪击事件幸存者正在起诉一家人工智能枪支检测公司，该公司的系统在事件中未能识别武器。该诉讼提出了对部署在公共安全应用中的AI系统的准确性标准和可靠性要求的重要疑问。

title: JMGO’s N3 Ultimate projector is the new portable 4K champ
summary: JMGO's N3 Ultimate is a portable 4K projector that excels at flexible placement and handling moderate ambient light, outperforming competing models like Anker's offerings. Priced at $2,399, the projector can rival more expensive home theater systems and justifies its premium cost through superior adaptability. The device represents a new benchmark for flagship portable projection technology.
content:
The N3 Ultimate doesn’t mind being off center. | Photo by Thomas Ricker / The Verge	

Sorry Anker: JMGO now makes my favorite flagship portable projector. 
The N3 Ultimate is an excellent portable 4K projector that defeats moderate ambient light at severe placement angles and can rival more expensive home theater installations at night. After a few weeks of testing, I think the raw adaptability exhibited by the JMGO's N3 Ultimate justifies its current $2,399 price ($500 off its $2,999 list).
Modern all-in-one projectors built around Google TV are already super accommodating when it comes to placement. Set one down on a living room table or campsite rock and it will begin searching for a screen or blank wall while avoiding o …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: JMGO N3 Ultimate投影仪是新一代便携式4K冠军
zh.summary: JMGO N3 Ultimate是一款便携式4K投影仪，在灵活放置和处理中等环境光线方面表现出色，超越了Anker等竞争对手的产品。价格为$2,399，该投影仪可以与更昂贵的家庭影院系统相媲美，其卓越的适应性为其高价格提供了正当理由。该设备代表了旗舰便携式投影技术的新基准。
